_doc: What CIVVIS does with each shipped Civ VI modifier EffectType.
A status describes whether CIVVIS' ENGINE can express the effect for the
content it models -- not whether every row's owning object exists here.
Content scope is measured separately, by the 'Only in Civ VI' columns of
tools/civ6_fidelity.py. An effect is 'implemented' only when a concrete
execution path exists; when in doubt the honest status is 'partial'.
These are inspection judgements, seeded by reading the engine for each
effect family. They are not yet verified row by row against the game.
Per-row verification is what turns this census into a proof, and it is the
next step; until then treat 'implemented' as a claim to be checked, not a
guarantee. Anything absent from this file counts as unmodelled, so newly
shipped content raises the backlog rather than hiding in it.

A 'verified' entry has had every shipped row drilled with --effect and matched against the CIVVIS path that covers it, or attributed to content CIVVIS does not model. Everything else is still an inspection judgement.

ATTACH_MODIFIER.note: The interpreter primitive: attach another modifier to a collection. CIVVIS hardcodes effects per object, so it has no general way to express this. Implementing it is phase 2 itself, and it gates the long tail behind it.

ATTACH_MODIFIER_TO_PLAYERTYPE.note: World Congress outcomes attaching modifiers to a class of players. Congress resolutions are hardcoded per resolution instead.

DIPLOMACY_SIMPLE_EFFECT.note: Bespoke leader agendas, which MECHANICS.md scopes out deliberately. Almost every row is a TraitModifier on a leader CIVVIS does not model.

ADJUST_PLAYER_STRENGTH_MODIFIER.note: Verified row by row. All 48 promotion Combat Strength amounts CIVVIS models match the shipped values exactly, under CIVVIS' own semantic effect names. The Giant Death Robot's four tech upgrades were attached to the wrong nodes: Advanced Power Cells ships the Particle Beam Siege Cannon and Cybernetics Enhanced Mobility, while CIVVIS had the movement on Advanced Power Cells and invented a healing upgrade on Cybernetics that no shipped row grants. Now corrected. The remaining rows are unit abilities for leaders and city-states outside CIVVIS' roster.

ADJUST_UNIT_MOVEMENT.note: Verified row by row with conditions resolved. Advanced Engines, Commando, Helmsman, Pursuit, Redeploy, Swift Keel each grant 1 and Roadies 4, all matching, and the Giant Death Robot's Enhanced Mobility grants 3 from Cybernetics. The rest are leader and city-state abilities, Commemorations, Emergencies and the Letters of Marque card, none of which CIVVIS carries.

ADJUST_UNIT_EXPERIENCE_MODIFIER.note: Verified row by row. Barracks, Armory, Basilikoi Paides, Lighthouse and Hangar all grant 25% to the units they train, matching. Two did not: After Action Reports is +50% and CIVVIS had 25, and Airport-trained aircraft gain 50% where CIVVIS had 25. Elite Forces (+100%) is a card CIVVIS does not carry.

ADJUST_UNIT_GRANT_EXPERIENCE.note: Flat XP grants on training and from Great People.

ADJUST_UNIT_HEALING_MODIFIERS.note: Verified row by row. Chaplain's +20 adjacent heal matches. Auxiliary Ships, Supply Fleet and Supercarrier do not let a ship heal as if it were home: each pays 5 in enemy territory and 10 in neutral. CIVVIS modelled all three as a boolean 'heal anywhere' worth the full 20, which roughly doubled naval sustain in neutral water and quadrupled it in enemy water.

ADJUST_UNIT_SPY_OPERATION_CHANCE.note: The espionage promotion tree adjusts operation odds.

ENABLE_UNIT_FAITH_PURCHASE.note: Verified row by row. Theocracy and the Grand Master's Chapel enable Faith purchase for exactly ten land combat classes -- melee, ranged, anti-cavalry, light/heavy cavalry, light/heavy chariot, ranged cavalry, recon and siege. CIVVIS admitted every land military unit, which also let Theocracy Faith-buy a Giant Death Robot; the GDR is its own class outside the list, and is now excluded. The naval and Archaeologist rows are leader traits, and the Spy row is Wu Zetian -- content CIVVIS does not model.

ADJUST_TRADE_ROUTE_CAPACITY.note: Verified row by row, and it found two gaps now fixed. A city earns up to two routes from its own infrastructure and each is an either/or: the Commercial Hub grants one and a Harbor grants it only where there is no Commercial Hub (REQUIRES_NO_COMMERCIAL_HUB), with England's Royal Navy Dockyard unconditional; separately a Market grants one and a Lighthouse only where there is no Market (REQUIRES_NO_MARKET). CIVVIS had only the building tier. Merchant Republic's two routes were also missing. Foreign Trade, Colossus, Great Zimbabwe, Carthage and the Great Merchants already executed. Alliance-level routes remain unmodelled.

ADJUST_GREAT_PERSON_POINTS.note: Verified row by row, and it found three wrong policy cards. Laissez-Faire is +4 Great Merchant empire-wide plus 2 per Bank, 4 per Stock Exchange, 4 Great Admiral per Seaport and 2 per Shipyard; Nobel Prize is +4 Great Scientist plus 2 per University, 4 per Research Lab, 2 Great Engineer per Factory and 4 per Power Plant; Military Organization was missing its flat +4 Great General. CIVVIS had collapsed the first two into one flat number per building tier. Strategos, Inspiration, Revelation, Literary Tradition, Navigation, Traveling Merchants, Frescoes, Invention and Symphonies were already exact. Divine Spark and the Commemoration/Zhangye Danxia rows are pantheon and content scope.

ADJUST_CITY_TOURISM.note: Verified row by row, all matching. Heritage Tourism doubles Artifact, Landscape, Portrait, Religious and Sculpture works and not Writing or Music, which is exactly CIVVIS' art-and-artifact grouping. Curator doubles all six object types. Cristo Redentor doubles Seaside Resort Tourism. Curator's placement looked wrong -- its id is GOVERNOR_PROMOTION_MERCHANT_CURATOR and Rise and Fall gives it to Reyna -- but Gathering Storm's override of Expansion1_Governors.xml, shipped inside the Expansion2 folder, moves it to Pingala while keeping the old id. CIVVIS has it on Pingala and is right.

ADJUST_DISTRICT_TOURISM_CHANGE.note: Verified row by row, all matching. Ferris Wheel 2, Shopping Mall 4, Thermal Bath 3 gated on a Geothermal Fissure in the city, Stadium 2 at 10 Population and 5 at 20, Aquatics Center 2 for a coastal Wonder, and Conservation's wall Tourism at 1/2/3 for Ancient, Medieval and Renaissance Walls with the Tsikhe at 3.

ADJUST_CITY_IDENTITY_PER_TURN.note: Verified row by row. Monument +1, Limitanei +2 with a garrison and Praetorium +2 with a Governor all match. Three did not: Martial Law pays 2 for a garrison and CIVVIS paid 4; Colonial Offices grants +3 Loyalty in cities off the Capital's continent, which CIVVIS omitted entirely while keeping the growth half; and the Colosseum's +2 Loyalty to every city within six tiles was missing, though its Culture, Amenities and range were present. Automated Workforce, Police State and the Government Plaza debuff belong to content CIVVIS does not carry.

ADJUST_CITY_FREE_POWER.note: Verified row by row: Geothermal Plant 4, Hydroelectric Dam 6, Wind/Solar/Offshore Wind 2 each, Reyna's Renewable Subsidizer +2 on each of those five, and Aerospace Contractors' Spaceport 3 all execute with the shipped amounts. The three remaining rows are Cardiff, a city-state outside CIVVIS' modelled roster, which is content scope rather than effect scope.

ADJUST_CITY_GREATWORK_YIELD.note: Great Work yields, slots and Culture/Tourism output execute.

ADJUST_PLAYER_GOVERNOR_POINTS.note: Verified row by row, and it found the biggest single gap yet. Fourteen civics each award a Governor title -- State Workforce, Early Empire, Defensive Tactics, Recorded History, Guilds, Medieval Faires, Civil Engineering, Nationalism, Mass Media, Mobilization, Globalization, Social Media, Near Future Governance and Future Civic. CIVVIS hardcoded three, two of which (Political Philosophy and Civil Service) award none in the shipped tree, so a full game yielded roughly a quarter of the titles it should. The grant is now a governor_title field on the civic. The Government Plaza district and its buildings award one each, and Casa de Contratacion three, all already present.
leads: Casa de Contratacion's three titles are modelled as governor_titles 3 on the wonder; confirm they arrive once rather than per building.

ADJUST_DISTRICT_YIELD_MODIFIER.note: Verified row by row. Every adjacency-doubling card CIVVIS carries matches at +100%: Aesthetics and Sports Media on the Theater Square, Natural Philosophy and Five Year Plan on the Campus, Five Year Plan and Craftsmen on the Industrial Zone, Town Charters and Economic Union on the Commercial Hub, Economic Union and Naval Infrastructure on the Harbor, Scripture on the Holy Site, and Reyna's Harbormaster on both Commercial Hub and Harbor. Collectivism is a card CIVVIS does not carry.

TERRAIN_ADJACENCY.note: District adjacency is audited source by source at zero divergence.

ADD_RELIGIOUS_BELIEF_YIELD.note: Verified, and the ambiguity is settled by the shipped text rather than the tables. Tithe, Pilgrimage and World Church each carry a base row and a Gathering Storm row with no Delete between them, but Gathering Storm also ships _EXPANSION2_DESCRIPTION wording for all three: Tithe '+3 Gold for each city following the Religion', Pilgrimage '+2 Faith for each city following this Religion', World Church '+1 Culture for every 4 followers of this Religion'. Only the Gathering Storm row is live, and 'each city following this Religion' means every such city rather than the founder's own. CIVVIS carries exactly that for all four beliefs, Church Property included. Reverting yesterday's change was correct.

ADJUST_BUILDING_PRODUCTION.note: Verified row by row. Limes doubles Castle and Star Fort Production, matching CIVVIS' wall card. Three Governor promotions each pair a headline effect with a Production bonus for one district's buildings, and CIVVIS implemented only the headline: Connoisseur adds 20% to Theater Square buildings alongside its Culture per Population, Divine Architect 20% to Holy Site buildings alongside Faith purchase, and Provision 20% to Industrial Zone buildings alongside settlers not consuming Population. All three now apply.
leads: Governor promotion-tree membership is settled by the grid slot, not the row list. GovernorPromotionSets is keyed on governor plus promotion and Gathering Storm never deletes the Rise and Fall rows, so the union over-counts; but every duplicate pair shares one (Level, Column), and the Gathering Storm promotion is the occupant. Reyna's L1C2 is Forestry Management over Foreign Exchange, Pingala's L3C2 is Space Initiative over Arms Race Proponent, Amani's L1C2 is Affluence over Prestige and L2C2 Foreign Investor over Promoter, Liang's L1C0 is Zoning Commissioner over Infrastructure and L2C2 Water Works over Architect. CIVVIS holds the Gathering Storm occupant of every slot.

ADJUST_PROJECT_PRODUCTION.note: Verified row by row. Space Initiative adds 30% to all five space-race projects, which CIVVIS carries as space_project_production_pct. The remaining rows are Arms Race Proponent's nuclear projects and the Rogue State card, neither of which CIVVIS models.

ADJUST_UNIT_TAG_ERA_PRODUCTION.note: Verified row by row. The amounts and class groupings were already right, but the effect's whole point is the era window and CIVVIS ignored it: Agoge boosted a Modern Infantry as readily as a Warrior, and every one of the nine cards stacked across all nine eras. Each card now carries its shipped window -- Agoge 0-1, Feudal Contract 2-3, Grande Armee 4-5, Military First 6-7, Chivalry 0-4, Lightning Warfare 5-7, Defense of the Motherland 5-7, Strategic Air Force 6-7, International Waters 0-7 -- checked against the era of the node that unlocks the unit. Letters of Marque, Patriotic War and Finest Hour are cards CIVVIS does not carry.

ADJUST_CITY_YIELD_MODIFIER.note: Verified row by row for the wonders, policies and governments CIVVIS models. Amundsen-Scott, Broadway, Casa de Contratacion and Kilwa Kisiwani carry their shipped percentages; Colonial Taxes was missing the +10% Production that accompanies its +25% Gold on foreign continents, now applied. Communism and Corporate Libertarianism yields, Disinformation Campaign and the Great Work rows remain content or systems CIVVIS scopes differently; POLICY_INQUISITION belongs to a game mode outside the baseline.

GRANT_UNIT_IN_CITY.note: Verified row by row against the wonders, beliefs and buildings CIVVIS models. Colossus, Pyramids, Statue of Zeus, Kotoku-in, Mahabodhi Temple, Meenakshi Temple, Fertility Rites, Religious Settlements and the tribal-village grants all execute with the shipped counts. The Statue of Liberty's two free Settlers were missing and are now granted. Remaining rows are leader traits, Government Plaza Spy/Builder grants and a Commemoration Giant Death Robot -- content CIVVIS does not model.

GRANT_YIELD.note: Instant yields from Great People activation and goody huts.

ADJUST_BUILDING_YIELD_CHANGE.note: Verified row by row. Third Alternative, Military Research, the Electronics Factory Culture, the Tsikhe Faith, Choral Music, Feed the World and Isaac Newton's University Science all match. City-state Envoy bonuses also match, and are the trap in this effect: the database ships TWO ladders. The base rows pay the Capital at 1 Envoy, the tier-1 building at 3 and the tier-2 building at 6, all at a flat 2. The Ethiopia-pack rows -- the final-patch structure CIVVIS implements, and the one a Gathering Storm game actually runs -- pay Capital and tier-1 at 1 Envoy, tier-2 plus the Consulate at 3, and tier-3 plus the Chancery at 6, with amounts 1/2/3 doubled for Trade. Reading the base rows and 'correcting' CIVVIS to them is wrong; the two are distinguished only by the _ETHIOPIA suffix on the attaching modifier. Still partial for the structural reason: an arbitrary modifier cannot adjust an arbitrary building's yield, only the named sources here.

ADJUST_PLOT_YIELD.note: Partly checked. Liang's City Park Culture and Fishery Production match. Egypt's Sphinx was missing both of its conditional yields -- +1 Culture on Floodplains and +2 Faith next to a Wonder -- now added. Still partial: arbitrary plot-yield grants have no general path.
leads: Resolved: the Lumber Mill's river bonus is superseded. Gathering Storm's shipped text reads '+2 Production. Can only be built on Woods. Once Mercantilism is unlocked it can also be built on Rainforest', with no river clause -- the base game's +1 base and +1 on rivers were folded into the flat 2, and the river modifier is left attached as dead data. CIVVIS' flat 2 was right. The same sentence turned up a real gap: CIVVIS allowed Lumber Mills on Rainforest from Construction, with no Mercantilism gate.

GRANT_ABILITY.note: Promotions and a fixed set of named unit abilities execute, but the general UnitAbilities system that most of these rows target does not exist. Most rows here are Great Person birth abilities.

CITY_GRANT_RANDOM_RESOURCE_PRODUCT.note: Products and Corporations exist and are capped and movable, but the project-completion random grant is not wired.
